translated: I never told the chief and the people to listen; the elders gathered to come and obey my commands and rules, but they say that their deeds are done by force. Now see what small matter is untrue because I came and said.

ref: Then he said to the servant in charge of the first herd, “When Esau meets you, he will ask whose servant you are. He will want to know where you are going and who owns those animals in front of you.
text: Kumo be kaman nɛ e kaŋɛ anya na to be emo nɛ e daŋ juŋkpar na fanɛ mo da Isɔɔ baŋ tu mo nseŋ bishi mo fanɛ, “Wanɛ be esa e la fo? Nnɛ nɛ fee yɔ? Wanɛ e wɔ asɔbɔya damta nɛ b juŋkpar fo ere kikɛ?”
translated: Kumo the chief saw with his eyes, and when the people went to the market, I went to Isɔɔ's house, then I heard this at my house: "Who did this thing? What do you want to say? Who is fighting with the chief's authority and the market people?"

ref: Seir was from the Horite tribe that had lived in Edom before the time of Esau. The clans that had descended from him were Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,
text: Seyir nɛ e la Horaiebi be esa na be mbinyɛnbi nɛ b daa wɔ efuli na so nde. Lotan nɛ Shobal nɛ Zibiɔn nɛ Ana,
translated: Seyir says that Horaiebi did that and was praised, so that they placed him in honor on that day. Lotan, Shobal, Zibiɔn, Ana

ref: God gave them his blessing and said: Have a lot of children! Fill the earth with people and bring it under your control. Rule over the fish in the ocean, the birds in the sky, and every animal on the earth.
text: Ndoŋ nna nɛ Ebɔrɛ nɛfa bumo nseŋ kaŋɛ bumo le: “Men kurge n wora keshi n salga to n sɔ kasawule so kikɛ nsaa ji eleŋ kumo so. Men baa ji eleŋ ekɔrɔtɔ so nɛ mbuibi so nɛ asɔ gbeyeso kikɛ nyam so.”
translated: The people of Ebɔrɛ say these things they speak thus: “We cultivate our fields and our harvest; the crops we plant, we hardly get enough of them. We even get enough of these root crops with these hoes and with much hard work.”

ref: Forty days later Noah opened a window to send out a raven, but it kept flying around until the water had dried up.
text: nseŋ lara nyiŋgbeŋ ashi kuloŋgboŋ na to n lɛ kowu nɛ k baa wɔtɔ a lɛa a lɛa a jo saŋɛ nɛ nchu na kikɛ nyam beeŋ feeto kasawule so kuraa.
translated: The people of Lara village, those who dwell, and they go where they sell their cows, to the market they go, they go, they state thus their words: yet eating food is not enough, this is what they do every day.

ref: Lot answered, “You have done us a great favor, sir. You have saved our lives, but please don't make us go to the hills. That's too far away. The city will be destroyed before we can get there, and we will be killed when it happens.
text: N wu fanɛ ma asheŋ bɔlɔ fo kenishi, ŋkpal manɛ so, fo wora ma kelela ga m mɔlga ma ŋkpa, ama abee na ase wɔ kufɔ ga. Amoso pɔɛŋ nɛ n fo ndoŋ kusɔ ko beeŋ tiŋ n wora ma nɛ n wu.
translated: I say that the chief should not do those things; the people are saying, you should advise me on my matter, and know that the work is good. Amoso says that what I do is not falsehood, what I hear and I speak.

ref: Then he said to the servant in charge of the first herd, “When Esau meets you, he will ask whose servant you are. He will want to know where you are going and who owns those animals in front of you.

translated: Hunter Kumo was looking at it with his eyes, and the animal he had held with the net, and when I saw the deer, then I asked before leaving, "Who is this you are catching? Why do you want to say? Who do you use this net so tightly with the trap to catch small creatures?"

ref: Seir was from the Horite tribe that had lived in Edom before the time of Esau. The clans that had descended from him were Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,
text: Seyir nɛ e la Horaiebi be esa na be mbinyɛnbi nɛ b daa wɔ efuli na so nde. Lotan nɛ Shobal nɛ Zibiɔn nɛ Ana,
translated: Seyir says that Horaiebi did it and that he was praised, so that they would place him in honor on that day. Lotan, Shobal, Zibiɔn, and Ana.

text: Ndoŋ nna nɛ Ebɔrɛ nɛfa bumo nseŋ kaŋɛ bumo le: “Men kurge n wora keshi n salga to n sɔ kasawule so kikɛ nsaa ji eleŋ kumo so. Men baa ji eleŋ ekɔrɔtɔ so nɛ mbuibi so nɛ asɔ gbeyeso kikɛ nyam so.”
translated: The people of Ebɔrɛ say these things they speak: “We till the land and plant yams, and with hard work, we get the harvest we grow. We only get hunger because of our laziness and because of not working, with our suffering.”

ref: Forty days later Noah opened a window to send out a raven, but it kept flying around until the water had dried up.

translated: The people of Lara village, the merchants, travel with their goods, carrying things repeatedly; they say in this market, and even buying food, there is so much suffering that they cry.

ref: Lot answered, “You have done us a great favor, sir. You have saved our lives, but please don't make us go to the hills. That's too far away. The city will be destroyed before we can get there, and we will be killed when it happens.
text: N wu fanɛ ma asheŋ bɔlɔ fo kenishi, ŋkpal manɛ so, fo wora ma kelela ga m mɔlga ma ŋkpa, ama abee na ase wɔ kufɔ ga. Amoso pɔɛŋ nɛ n fo ndoŋ kusɔ ko beeŋ tiŋ n wora ma nɛ n wu.
translated: I say don’t let the fire burn now, for people live here; don’t let them bring my sickness to my hands, and know that the work is in the mouth. Always speak the true words I say, not false ones that come from my saying.

ref: Then he said to the servant in charge of the first herd, “When Esau meets you, he will ask whose servant you are. He will want to know where you are going and who owns those animals in front of you.
text: Kumo be kaman nɛ e kaŋɛ anya na to be emo nɛ e daŋ juŋkpar na fanɛ mo da Isɔɔ baŋ tu mo nseŋ bishi mo fanɛ, “Wanɛ be esa e la fo? Nnɛ nɛ fee yɔ? Wanɛ e wɔ asɔbɔya damta nɛ b juŋkpar fo ere kikɛ?”
translated: People say that what they see with their eyes and what they do is that they bring junk to the market where I go, and when I stand there in my place, I ask: "Who are you carrying this thing for? What do you want to say? Who is it because of this junk that we should sell?"

translated: Seyir says that Horaiebi and the youths and elders are placing (it) on the altar and this day. Lotan, Shobal, Zibiɔn, and Ana
